question: [File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: CryptoAllowanceController.getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pagination link generation in `getPaginationLink()` to craft a `next` URL that skips the `amountCondition > 0` filter by directly constructing a request with a `spender.id` value that bypasses the zero-amount exclusion, exposing revoked fee authorizations?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: router.getExt(getPath('allowances/crypto'), CryptoAllowanceController.getAccountCryptoAllowances)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `limit=1` parameter and iterate through all pages of crypto allowances using the `next` pagination link to enumerate all fee-transfer authorizations for a target account, one record at a time, without triggering any rate limit?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: AccountController.extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply multiple `spender.id=eq:X` filters to the `/nfts` endpoint to trigger the `spenderIdInFilters` aggregation, causing a SQL IN-clause with many values that retrieves NFT fee-approval records for a large set of spenders in a single query?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: EntityService.getEncodedId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a malformed EVM address (e.g., `0x` followed by 39 hex chars) as `idOrAliasOrEvmAddress` to trigger an `InvalidArgumentError` with a message that leaks internal entity ID parsing logic, enabling inference of valid fee-collector account addresses?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: router.getExt(getPath('tokens'), TokenController.getTokenRelationships)] Can an unprivileged external user query `/accounts/:id/tokens` for a known fee-schedule file account (e.g., 0.0.111) to enumerate all token relationships, revealing which tokens have custom fee schedules associated with that system account?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: BaseController.getLowerFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `spender.id=gte:X` filter without a corresponding upper bound to the `/allowances/tokens` endpoint, causing the lower-bound-only query path in `getLowerFilters()` to return all token fee allowances above a threshold, enabling fee-allowance enumeration from a known starting point?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: AccountController.listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply multiple `timestamp=eq:X` filters to the `/rewards` endpoint to retrieve staking rewards (fee-adjacent) at specific consensus timestamps, enabling correlation of fee events with specific transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getPath()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a shard.realm.num format account ID (e.g., `0.0.98`) as `idOrAliasOrEvmAddress` to access the treasury account's fee-related allowances and token relationships, leaking the fee-collection structure of the network?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: CryptoAllowanceController.extractCryptoAllowancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `spender.id=gt:0` to the `/allowances/crypto` endpoint to retrieve all non-zero crypto allowances for a target account in descending order, effectively dumping all fee-transfer authorizations sorted by spender ID?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: TokenAllowanceController.getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `token.id` corresponding to a known fee-denomination token (e.g., a stablecoin used for custom fees) to the `/allowances/tokens` endpoint to enumerate all accounts that have granted fee-payment allowances in that token?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: BaseController.getUpperFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `spender.id=lte:X` and `token.id=lte:Y` to the `/allowances/tokens` endpoint to trigger the upper-bound query path in `getUpperFilters()`, retrieving all token fee allowances below specific thresholds and enabling systematic fee-allowance mapping?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: router.getExt(getPath('nfts'), AccountController.getNftsByAccountId)] Can an unprivileged external user supply `serialnumber=gte:1` and `token.id=gte:0.0.1` to the `/nfts` endpoint to retrieve all NFTs with fee-approval flags for a target account, mapping the complete NFT fee-approval landscape?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: extendExpress() _once()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a double-call scenario in the `_once()` wrapper by sending a request that causes both the normal response path and the error path to attempt to call `next()`, causing the second call to be silently dropped and fee-related error handling to be bypassed?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: AccountController.validateFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user supply more than one `spender.id=lte:X` filter to the `/nfts` endpoint to trigger the `Multiple range params not allowed` error in `validateFilters()`, and use the error response timing to infer whether the target account has NFT fee-approval relationships?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: CryptoAllowanceController.getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `spender.id` value equal to a known fee-collector account (e.g., 0.0.98) to the `/allowances/crypto` endpoint to check whether a target account has granted fee-collection rights to the treasury, leaking fee-authorization state?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: router.getExt(getPath('allowances/tokens'), TokenAllowanceController.getAccountTokenAllowances)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `spender.id=eq:0.0.98` filter to the `/allowances/tokens` endpoint to check whether a target account has granted token fee-collection rights to the treasury account, revealing fee-authorization relationships?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: BaseController.getPaginationLink()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the `bounds.next` value in the pagination link for the `/allowances/tokens` endpoint to craft a request that skips ahead in the fee-allowance dataset, bypassing sequential pagination and accessing fee records out of order?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: AccountController.extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `timestamp=gte:X&timestamp=lte:Y` range to the `/rewards` endpoint to retrieve staking rewards within a specific time window, correlating reward amounts (fee-adjacent) with known transaction timestamps to infer fee-charging patterns?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: TokenController.extractTokensRelationshipQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `token.id=ne:X` to the `/tokens` endpoint and observe the `InvalidArgumentError` thrown by `extractTokensRelationshipQuery()`, using the error to confirm whether a specific token (used for custom fees) is associated with the target account?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: router.getExt(getPath('allowances/crypto'), CryptoAllowanceController.getAccountCryptoAllowances)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `limit=0` parameter to the `/allowances/crypto` endpoint to bypass the default limit and retrieve zero records while still generating a `next` pagination link, enabling fee-allowance existence probing without data disclosure?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: EntityService.getEncodedId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a long-zero EVM address (e.g., `0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000062`) as `idOrAliasOrEvmAddress` to resolve to account 0.0.98 (treasury/fee-collector) and access its crypto allowances, leaking fee-collection authorization data?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: CryptoAllowanceController.extractCryptoAllowancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply multiple `spender.id=gte:X` and `spender.id=lte:Y` range filters to the `/allowances/crypto` endpoint to retrieve all crypto fee allowances within a specific spender ID range, enabling systematic fee-authorization mapping?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: AccountController.getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `token.id=gte:0.0.1&serialnumber=gte:1` to the `/nfts` endpoint to retrieve all NFTs with fee-approval flags across all tokens for a target account, mapping the complete fee-approval NFT landscape in a single paginated scan?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: BaseController.updateQueryFiltersWithInValues()] Can an unprivileged external user supply the maximum allowed number of repeated `spender.id=eq:X` parameters (up to `maxRepeatedQueryParameters`) to the `/allowances/crypto` endpoint to generate a SQL IN-clause that retrieves fee allowances for a large batch of spenders in a single query, bypassing per-request data limits?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: router.getExt(getPath('rewards'), AccountController.listStakingRewardsByAccountId)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `timestamp=eq:X` filter matching a known high-fee transaction timestamp to the `/rewards` endpoint to retrieve staking rewards at that exact moment, correlating fee events with reward distributions?
